## Solution

**Step 1 — New Part C reliability (backup supplier):**

P(C fails) = P(primary fails) × P(backup fails) = 0.12 × 0.15 = 0.018
P(C available) = 1 − 0.018 = **0.982**

**Step 2 — New assembly rate (all 5 parts):**

P = 0.95 × 0.92 × 0.982 × 0.97 × 0.90

- 0.95 × 0.92 = 0.8740
- × 0.982 = 0.85827
- × 0.97 = 0.83252
- × 0.90 = **0.74927 ≈ 74.9%**

The backup supplier boosts Part C from 88% to 98.2%, but adding Part E at 90% claws most of that gain back — the two changes nearly cancel out.

**Answer: (A) 74.9%**
